name: Google Sheets API
what_it_does: Google Sheets API v4 — read, write, format, and manage Google Sheets spreadsheets programmatically, enabling agents to use Sheets as a lightweight database or reporting surface.
use_cases: Agents writing structured results (reports, logs, analysis output) to a shared Google Sheet
Reading configuration or input data from a Sheets spreadsheet maintained by non-technical users
Batch appending rows of data from agent pipelines into shared tracking sheets
Creating formatted reports with charts, colors, and formulas via the Sheets API
Using Google Sheets as a lightweight CMS or config source for agent-driven workflows
not_for: High-frequency writes or large datasets — Sheets is a spreadsheet tool, not a database; use PostgreSQL for structured data at scale
Real-time data access — Sheets API has rate limits and latency not suitable for real-time agent queries
Private data that shouldn't leave Google — all data goes through Google servers
best_when: Non-technical stakeholders maintain data in Sheets and agents need to read from or write to that shared spreadsheet without requiring database access.
avoid_when: You need a real database with transactions, indexing, or high-throughput writes — Sheets is for human-readable collaborative documents, not structured storage.

auth.notes: Google OAuth2 or service account. Scopes: sheets.readonly for read, sheets for write. Service account must be shared on the specific spreadsheet to access it.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free with a Google account; Sheets API is free with 60 requests/minute per user
pricing.paid_tiers: Google Workspace Business plans from $6/user/month add enterprise features
pricing.notes: Sheets API itself is free. Rate limits apply. Google Workspace required for some enterprise features.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests per 100 seconds per user; 500 requests per 100 seconds per project

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard Google API errors. QUOTA_EXCEEDED errors clear with specific retry-after guidance. Invalid range A1 notation returns clear 400 errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent. Append is not idempotent — retries create duplicate rows. Update by cell range is idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Service account email must be explicitly shared on each spreadsheet — not inheriting from folder sharing
Rate limit of 60 requests/minute per user is surprisingly easy to hit with bulk operations — use batchUpdate
Append adds rows at end of data range, not necessarily at end of sheet — row count grows unpredictably
A1 notation for ranges is required (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:C10') — dynamic range construction is error-prone
Sheets formulas are recalculated on read — reading a cell with =NOW() returns different values each read

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth scopes limit to read vs read-write. Service account credentials must be protected. Data goes through Google infrastructure. SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001 certified. Sheets are only as private as sharing settings.
